title: Improved table handling
author: delcon
body:
## Summary

I found several issues in data-table handling in worksheets regarding existing tables:
- loading existing Tables from workbook errors and alters table
- hidden header rows in Tables corrupt file on save
- totals row toggles on file save -> corrupt file on save

in addition to that I missed following features:
- define / create a table over existing data in Worksheet
- delete a table in Worksheets
  
## Updates

- fixed all errors reading existing Tables
- added ability to define Table over existing Data
- added Documentation for deleting a Table (function existed)
- added Documentation for defining a Table

## Test plan
I added a test to create a table, save it, read it back and comparing them to some extend
However, valid tests would also include reading generated Table in Excel and visually comparing them and creating Tables in Excel and reading them with the library

## Other thoughts
- I am not sure if current table data handling (duplicating worksheet data) is useful
- I am not sure if current requirement to commit table data changes is necessary useful

let me know what you think

comments.author: papb
comments.body:
Hello! I am currently having an issue in which I cannot have a column header with a name with a tab or newline (e.g. `Foo\tBar` or `Foo\nBar`), it says `Excel found unreadable content` and asks if I want to recover. I saw your PR, I was wondering, does your PR fix this? If not, do you have any idea on how to fix this?

Thank you :grimacing:

comments.author: delcon
comments.body:
> Hello! I am currently having an issue in which I cannot have a column header with a name with a tab or newline (e.g. `Foo\tBar` or `Foo\nBar`), it says `Excel found unreadable content` and asks if I want to recover. I saw your PR, I was wondering, does your PR fix this? If not, do you have any idea on how to fix this?
> 
> Thank you 😬

Probably not. My fixes concern Data Tables in Excel.
.xlsx files are .zip files. Looking at a string generated by Excel, I see that a CR/LF is required for a new line.

comments.author: delcon
comments.body: Hey exceljs, any feedback on my pullrequest? And how can I fix the npm error?

comments.author: papb
comments.body:
Hi @delcon, I am not part of the exceljs team but I have some suggestions:

* Fix the conflict in README.md
* There are three tests failing with your PR, you can see the first [here](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/pull/1345/checks?check_run_id=787727802#step:8:1373) and just scroll down to see the other two. Apparently your PR caused the `totalsRowShown` and `ref` fields to disappear in the generated XML.

comments.author: RW-DiefBell
comments.body: @delcon any progress on this? I'm having issues with loading existing tables from worksheets and I think this may fix it

comments.author: delcon
comments.body:
> @delcon any progress on this? I'm having issues with loading existing tables from worksheets and I think this may fix it

There doesn't seem to be much interst for my pull request. I stopped working on it. Your can clone my repo and see if it works out for you. Let me know If it works.

comments.author: RW-DiefBell
comments.body:
@delcon It's working fine for me, thanks for doing this. It does however break if your table has no rows or only one row. So I've got to put in two blank rows, which means the final file has two empty rows at the end of it. Any ideas regarding a fix?

I might help out with it if I have the time but I hate doing npm packages that aren't in TypeScript...
